contents: Require Import Basics.Overture Basics.Nat Basics.Tactics Basics.Decidable Basics.Equivalences Basics.PathGroupoids Types.Paths Types.Universe.
Require Import Basics.Overture Basics.Nat Basics.Tactics Basics.Decidable Basics.Equivalences Basics.PathGroupoids Types.Paths Types.Universe.

(** * The Integers *)

(** ** Definition *)

(** We define the integers as two copies of [nat] stuck together around a [zero]. *)

Inductive Int : Type0 :=
| negS : nat -> Int
| zero : Int
| posS : nat -> Int.

(** We can convert a [nat] to an [Int] by mapping [0] to [zero] and [S n] to [posS n]. Various operations on [nat] are preserved by this function. See the section on conversion functions starting with [int_nat_succ]. *)

Definition int_of_nat (n : nat) :=
  match n with
  | O => zero
  | S n => posS n
  end.
(** We declare this conversion as a coercion so we can freely use [nat]s in statements about integers. *)

(** ** Number Notations *)

(** Here we define some printing and parsing functions that convert the integers between numeral representations so that we can use notations such as [123] for [posS 122] and [-123] for [negS 122]. *)

(** Printing *)

Definition int_to_number_int (n : Int) : Numeral.int :=
  match n with
  | posS n => Numeral.IntDec (Decimal.Pos (Nat.to_uint (S n)))
  | zero => Numeral.IntDec (Decimal.Pos (Nat.to_uint 0))
  | negS n => Numeral.IntDec (Decimal.Neg (Nat.to_uint (S n)))
  end.

Definition int_of_number_int (d : Numeral.int) :=
  match d with
  | Numeral.IntDec (Decimal.Pos d) => int_of_nat (Nat.of_uint d)
  | Numeral.IntDec (Decimal.Neg d) => negS (nat_pred (Nat.of_uint d))
  | Numeral.IntHex (Hexadecimal.Pos u) => int_of_nat (Nat.of_hex_uint u)
  | Numeral.IntHex (Hexadecimal.Neg u) => negS (nat_pred (Nat.of_hex_uint u))
  end.
Number Notation Int int_of_number_int int_to_number_int : int_scope.

(** ** Successor, Predecessor and Negation *)

(** These operations will be used in the induction principle we derive for [Int] so we need to define them early on. *)

(** *** Successor and Predecessor *)

Definition int_succ (n : Int) : Int :=
  match n with
  | posS n => posS (S n)
  | 0 => 1
  | -1 => 0
  | negS (S n) => negS n
  end.

Definition int_pred (n : Int) : Int :=
  match n with
  | posS (S n) => posS n
  | 1 => 0 
  | 0 => -1
  | negS n => negS (S n)
  end.

Definition int_neg@{} (x : Int) : Int :=
  match x with
  | posS x => negS x
  | zero => zero
  | negS x => posS x
  end.

(** ** Basic Properties *)

(** *** Integer induction *)

(** The induction principle for integers is similar to the induction principle for natural numbers. However we have two induction hypotheses going in either direction starting from 0. *)

Definition Int_ind@{i} (P : Int -> Type@{i})
  (H0 : P 0)
  (HP : forall n : nat, P n -> P (int_succ n))
  (HN : forall n : nat, P (- n) -> P (int_pred (-n)))
  : forall x, P x.

hypotheses.type: forall n : nat, P n -> P n.+1
forall n : nat, P (- n) -> P (- n).-1
forall n : nat, P n -> P n.+1
forall n : nat, P (- n) -> P (- n).-1
forall n : nat, P n -> P n.+1
forall n : nat, P (- n) -> P (- n).-1
forall n : nat, P n -> P n.+1

forall n : nat, P n -> P n.+1

forall n : nat, P n -> P n.+1

forall n : nat, P n -> P n.+1

forall n : nat, P n -> P n.+1

forall n : nat, P (- n) -> P (- n).-1
forall n : nat, P n -> P n.+1
forall n : nat, P (- n) -> P (- n).-1
forall n : nat, P n -> P n.+1
forall n : nat, P (- n) -> P (- n).-1

contents: (** We record these so that they can be used with the [induction] tactic. *)

(** *** Decidable Equality *)

(** The integers have decidable equality. *)
destruct x as [x | | x], y as [y |  | y].

goals.conclusion: negS x = negS y -> x = y
posS x = posS y -> x = y

contents: (** By Hedberg's theorem, we have that the integers are a set. *)

(** *** Pointedness *)

(** We sometimes want to treat the integers as a pointed type with basepoint given by 0. *)

(** ** Operations *)

(** *** Addition *)

(** Addition for integers is defined by integer induction on the first argument. *)
induction x as [|x IHx|x IHx] in y |- *.

(** *** Multiplication *)

(** Multiplication for integers is defined by integer induction on the first argument. *)
induction x as [|x IHx|x IHx] in y |- *.

(** ** Properties of Operations *)

(** *** Negation *)

(** Negation is involutive. *)

Definition isinj_int_neg@{} (x y : Int) : - x = - y -> x = y
  := equiv_inj int_neg.
(** The negation of a successor is the predecessor of the negation. *)
(** The negation of a predecessor is the successor of the negation. *)
(** The successor of a predecessor is the identity. *)
(** The predecessor of a successor is the identity. *)
(** The successor is an equivalence on [Int] *)

Instance isequiv_int_succ@{} : IsEquiv int_succ
  := isequiv_adjointify int_succ int_pred int_pred_succ int_succ_pred.
(** The predecessor is an equivalence on [Int] *)

Instance isequiv_int_pred@{} : IsEquiv int_pred
  := isequiv_inverse int_succ.

(** *** Addition *)

(** Integer addition with zero on the left is the identity by definition. *)
(** Integer addition with zero on the right is the identity. *)
(** Adding a successor on the left is the successor of the sum. *)
Definition int_add_succ_l@{} (x y : Int) : x.+1 + y = (x + y).+1.
induction x as [|[|x] IHx|[|x] IHx] in y |- *.

hypotheses.type: forall y0 : Int, x.+1%nat.+1 + y0 = (x.+1%nat + y0).+1
forall y0 : Int, (- x.+1%nat).+1 + y0 = (- x.+1%nat + y0).+1
forall y0 : Int, (- x.+1%nat).+1 + y0 = (- x.+1%nat + y0).+1

contents: (** Adding a predecessor on the left is the predecessor of the sum. *)
Definition int_add_pred_l@{} (x y : Int) : x.-1 + y = (x + y).-1.
induction x as [|[|x] IHx|[|x] IHx] in y |- *.

hypotheses.type: forall y0 : Int, x.+1%nat.-1 + y0 = (x.+1%nat + y0).-1
forall y0 : Int, (- x.+1%nat).-1 + y0 = (- x.+1%nat + y0).-1
forall y0 : Int, x.+1%nat.-1 + y0 = (x.+1%nat + y0).-1

contents: (** Adding a successor on the right is the successor of the sum. *)
Definition int_add_succ_r@{} (x y : Int) : x + y.+1 = (x + y).+1.
induction x as [|x IHx|[] IHx] in y |- *.

hypotheses.type: forall y0 : Int, x + y0.+1 = (x + y0).+1
forall y0 : Int, - n.+1%nat + y0.+1 = (- n.+1%nat + y0).+1
forall y0 : Int, x + y0.+1 = (x + y0).+1
forall y0 : Int, - n.+1%nat + y0.+1 = (- n.+1%nat + y0).+1
forall y0 : Int, - n.+1%nat + y0.+1 = (- n.+1%nat + y0).+1
forall y0 : Int, - n.+1%nat + y0.+1 = (- n.+1%nat + y0).+1
forall y0 : Int, - n.+1%nat + y0.+1 = (- n.+1%nat + y0).+1
forall y0 : Int, - n.+1%nat + y0.+1 = (- n.+1%nat + y0).+1
forall y0 : Int, - n.+1%nat + y0.+1 = (- n.+1%nat + y0).+1

contents: (** Adding a predecessor on the right is the predecessor of the sum. *)
Definition int_add_pred_r (x y : Int) : x + y.-1 = (x + y).-1.
induction x as [|x IHx|[] IHx] in y |- *.

hypotheses.type: forall y0 : Int, x + y0.-1 = (x + y0).-1
forall y0 : Int, - n.+1%nat + y0.-1 = (- n.+1%nat + y0).-1
forall y0 : Int, x + y0.-1 = (x + y0).-1
forall y0 : Int, x + y0.-1 = (x + y0).-1

forall y0 : Int, - n.+1%nat + y0.-1 = (- n.+1%nat + y0).-1
forall y0 : Int, - n.+1%nat + y0.-1 = (- n.+1%nat + y0).-1

contents: Definition int_add_comm@{} (x y : Int) : x + y = y + x.
induction y as [|y IHy|y IHy] in x |- *.

hypotheses.type: forall x0 : Int, x0 + y = y + x0
forall x0 : Int, x0 + - y = - y + x0
forall x0 : Int, x0 + y = y + x0
forall x0 : Int, x0 + y = y + x0
forall x0 : Int, x0 + y = y + x0
forall x0 : Int, x0 + - y = - y + x0
forall x0 : Int, x0 + - y = - y + x0
forall x0 : Int, x0 + - y = - y + x0

contents: Definition int_add_assoc@{} (x y z : Int) : x + (y + z) = x + y + z.
induction x as [|x IHx|x IHx] in y, z |- *.

hypotheses.type: forall y0 z0 : Int, x + (y0 + z0) = x + y0 + z0
forall y0 z0 : Int, - x + (y0 + z0) = - x + y0 + z0
forall y0 z0 : Int, x + (y0 + z0) = x + y0 + z0
forall y0 z0 : Int, - x + (y0 + z0) = - x + y0 + z0

contents: (** Negation is a left inverse with respect to integer addition. *)
by rewrite int_neg_succ, int_add_pred_l, int_add_succ_r, IHx.
by rewrite int_neg_pred, int_add_succ_l, int_add_pred_r, IHx.
(** Negation is a right inverse with respect to integer addition. *)
Definition int_neg_add@{} (x y : Int) : - (x + y) = - x - y.
induction x as [|x IHx|x IHx] in y |- *.

hypotheses.type: forall y0 : Int, - (x + y0) = - x + - y0
forall y0 : Int, - (- x + y0) = - - x + - y0
forall y0 : Int, - (x + y0) = - x + - y0
forall y0 : Int, - (x + y0) = - x + - y0
forall y0 : Int, - (x + y0) = - x + - y0
forall y0 : Int, - (x + y0) = - x + - y0
forall y0 : Int, - (- x + y0) = - - x + - y0
forall y0 : Int, - (- x + y0) = - - x + - y0
forall y0 : Int, - (- x + y0) = - - x + - y0
forall y0 : Int, - (- x + y0) = - - x + - y0
forall y0 : Int, - (- x + y0) = - - x + - y0

contents:
(** *** Multiplication *)

(** Multiplication with a successor on the left is the sum of the multiplication without the successor and the multiplicand which was not a successor. *)
Definition int_mul_succ_l@{} (x y : Int) : x.+1 * y = y + x * y.
induction x as [|[|x] IHx|[] IHx] in y |- *.

hypotheses.type: forall y0 : Int, 0%nat.+1 * y0 = y0 + 0%nat * y0
forall y0 : Int, x.+1%nat.+1 * y0 = y0 + x.+1%nat * y0
forall y0 : Int, (- 0%nat).+1 * y0 = y0 + - 0%nat * y0
forall y0 : Int, (- n.+1%nat).+1 * y0 = y0 + - n.+1%nat * y0
forall y0 : Int, 0%nat.+1 * y0 = y0 + 0%nat * y0
forall y0 : Int, x.+1%nat.+1 * y0 = y0 + x.+1%nat * y0
forall y0 : Int, (- 0%nat).+1 * y0 = y0 + - 0%nat * y0
forall y0 : Int, (- 0%nat).+1 * y0 = y0 + - 0%nat * y0
forall y0 : Int, (- 0%nat).+1 * y0 = y0 + - 0%nat * y0
forall y0 : Int, (- n.+1%nat).+1 * y0 = y0 + - n.+1%nat * y0
forall y0 : Int, (- n.+1%nat).+1 * y0 = y0 + - n.+1%nat * y0

goals.conclusion:
nat_rect (fun _ : nat => Int -> Int) (fun y0 : Int => - y0 + 0)
  (fun (_ : nat) (IHx0 : Int -> Int) (y0 : Int) => - y0 + IHx0 y0) n y =
y +
(- y +
 nat_rect (fun _ : nat => Int -> Int) (fun y0 : Int => - y0 + 0)
   (fun (_ : nat) (IHx0 : Int -> Int) (y0 : Int) => - y0 + IHx0 y0) n y)

hypotheses.type: forall y0 : Int, (- n.+1%nat).+1 * y0 = y0 + - n.+1%nat * y0

goals.conclusion:
nat_rect (fun _ : nat => Int -> Int) (fun y0 : Int => - y0 + 0)
  (fun (_ : nat) (IHx0 : Int -> Int) (y0 : Int) => - y0 + IHx0 y0) n y =
y + - y +
nat_rect (fun _ : nat => Int -> Int) (fun y0 : Int => - y0 + 0)
  (fun (_ : nat) (IHx0 : Int -> Int) (y0 : Int) => - y0 + IHx0 y0) n y

hypotheses.type: forall y0 : Int, (- n.+1%nat).+1 * y0 = y0 + - n.+1%nat * y0

goals.conclusion:
nat_rect (fun _ : nat => Int -> Int) (fun y0 : Int => - y0 + 0)
  (fun (_ : nat) (IHx0 : Int -> Int) (y0 : Int) => - y0 + IHx0 y0) n y =
0 +
nat_rect (fun _ : nat => Int -> Int) (fun y0 : Int => - y0 + 0)
  (fun (_ : nat) (IHx0 : Int -> Int) (y0 : Int) => - y0 + IHx0 y0) n y

hypotheses.type: forall y0 : Int, (- n.+1%nat).+1 * y0 = y0 + - n.+1%nat * y0

contents: (** Similarly, multiplication with a predecessor on the left is the sum of the multiplication without the predecessor and the negation of the multiplicand which was not a predecessor. *)
Definition int_mul_pred_l@{} (x y : Int) : x.-1 * y = -y + x * y.
induction x as [|x IHx|[] IHx] in y |- *.

hypotheses.type: forall y0 : Int, x.-1 * y0 = - y0 + x * y0
forall y0 : Int, (- 0%nat).-1 * y0 = - y0 + - 0%nat * y0
forall y0 : Int, (- n.+1%nat).-1 * y0 = - y0 + - n.+1%nat * y0
forall y0 : Int, x.-1 * y0 = - y0 + x * y0
forall y0 : Int, x.-1 * y0 = - y0 + x * y0
forall y0 : Int, x.-1 * y0 = - y0 + x * y0

goals.conclusion: x * y = - y + y + x * y

hypotheses.type: forall y0 : Int, x.-1 * y0 = - y0 + x * y0
forall y0 : Int, (- 0%nat).-1 * y0 = - y0 + - 0%nat * y0
forall y0 : Int, (- n.+1%nat).-1 * y0 = - y0 + - n.+1%nat * y0

contents: (** Integer multiplication with zero on the left is zero by definition. *)
(** Integer multiplication with zero on the right is zero. *)
(** Integer multiplication with one on the left is the identity. *)
(** Integer multiplication with one on the right is the identity. *)
(** Multiplying with a negation on the left is the same as negating the product. *)
Definition int_mul_neg_l@{} (x y : Int) : - x * y = - (x * y).
induction x as [|x IHx|x IHx] in y |- *.

hypotheses.type: forall y0 : Int, - x * y0 = - (x * y0)
forall y0 : Int, - - x * y0 = - (- x * y0)
forall y0 : Int, - x * y0 = - (x * y0)

forall y0 : Int, - - x * y0 = - (- x * y0)

contents: (** Multiplying with a successor on the right is the sum of the multiplication without the successor and the product of the multiplicand which was not a successor and the multiplicand. *)
Definition int_mul_succ_r@{} (x y : Int) : x * y.+1 = x + x * y.
induction x as [|x IHx|x IHx] in y |- *.

hypotheses.type: forall y0 : Int, x * y0.+1 = x + x * y0
forall y0 : Int, - x * y0.+1 = - x + - x * y0
forall y0 : Int, x * y0.+1 = x + x * y0

forall y0 : Int, x * y0.+1 = x + x * y0
forall y0 : Int, x * y0.+1 = x + x * y0

goals.conclusion: y + (x + x * y) = x + (y + x * y)

contents: (** Multiplying with a predecessor on the right is the sum of the multiplication without the predecessor and the product of the multiplicand which was not a predecessor and the negation of the multiplicand which was not a predecessor. *)
Definition int_mul_pred_r@{} (x y : Int) : x * y.-1 = -x + x * y.
induction x as [|x IHx|x IHx] in y |- *.

contents: Definition int_mul_comm@{} (x y : Int) : x * y = y * x.
induction y as [|y IHy|y IHy] in x |- *.

forall x0 : Int, x0 * - y = - y * x0

contents: (** Multiplying with a negation on the right is the same as negating the product. *)
Definition int_mul_neg_r@{} (x y : Int) : x * - y = - (x * y).
(** Multiplication distributes over addition on the left. *)
Definition int_dist_l@{} (x y z : Int) : x * (y + z) = x * y + x * z.

goals.conclusion: x * (y + z) = x * y + x * z
x * (y + z) = x * y + x * z

hypotheses.type: forall y0 z0 : Int, - x * (y0 + z0) = - x * y0 + - x * z0

contents: (** Multiplication distributes over addition on the right. *)
Definition int_dist_r@{} (x y z : Int) : (x + y) * z = x * z + y * z.

goals.conclusion: (x + y) * z = x * z + y * z
(x + y) * z = x * z + y * z

contents: Definition int_mul_assoc@{} (x y z : Int) : x * (y * z) = x * y * z.
induction x as [|x IHx|x IHx] in y, z |- *.

hypotheses.type: forall y0 z0 : Int, x * (y0 * z0) = x * y0 * z0
forall y0 z0 : Int, - x * (y0 * z0) = - x * y0 * z0
forall y0 z0 : Int, x * (y0 * z0) = x * y0 * z0

goals.conclusion: y * z + x * (y * z) = (y + x * y) * z

hypotheses.type: forall y0 z0 : Int, x * (y0 * z0) = x * y0 * z0

goals.conclusion: y * z + x * (y * z) = y * z + x * y * z

forall y0 z0 : Int, - x * (y0 * z0) = - x * y0 * z0

goals.conclusion: - y * z + - x * (y * z) = - y * z + - x * y * z

hypotheses.type: forall y0 z0 : Int, - x * (y0 * z0) = - x * y0 * z0

contents:
(** ** Iteration of equivalences *)

(** *** Iteration by arbitrary integers *)

(** Iteration by arbitrary integers requires the endofunction to be an equivalence, so that we can define a negative iteration by using its inverse. *)

Definition int_iter {A} (f : A -> A) `{!IsEquiv f} (n : Int) : A -> A
  := match n with
      | negS n => fun x => nat_iter n.+1%nat f^-1 x
      | zero => idmap
      | posS n => fun x => nat_iter n.+1%nat f x
     end.

Definition int_iter_neg {A} (f : A -> A) `{IsEquiv _ _ f} (n : Int) (a : A)
  : int_iter f (- n) a = int_iter f^-1 n a.

goals.conclusion: int_iter f (- n) a = int_iter f^-1 n a
int_iter f (- n) a = int_iter f^-1 n a

contents:
Definition int_iter_succ_l {A} (f : A -> A) `{IsEquiv _ _ f} (n : Int) (a : A)
  : int_iter f (int_succ n) a = f (int_iter f n a).

goals.conclusion: int_iter f n.+1 a = f (int_iter f n a)
int_iter f n.+1 a = f (int_iter f n a)
int_iter f 0.+1 a = f (int_iter f 0 a)
int_iter f n.+1.+1 a = f (int_iter f n.+1 a)

hypotheses.type: int_iter f n.+1 a = f (int_iter f n a)

goals.conclusion: int_iter f (- n).-1.+1 a = f (int_iter f (- n).-1 a)

hypotheses.type: int_iter f (- n).+1 a = f (int_iter f (- n) a)

goals.conclusion: int_iter f 0.+1 a = f (int_iter f 0 a)

goals.conclusion: int_iter f (- n) a = f (int_iter f (- n).-1 a)

hypotheses.type: int_iter f (- n).+1 a = f (int_iter f (- n) a)

goals.conclusion: int_iter f (- 0%nat) a = f (int_iter f (- 0%nat).-1 a)

hypotheses.type: int_iter f (- 0%nat).+1 a = f (int_iter f (- 0%nat) a)

goals.conclusion: int_iter f (- n.+1%nat) a = f (int_iter f (- n.+1%nat).-1 a)

hypotheses.type: int_iter f (- n.+1%nat).+1 a = f (int_iter f (- n.+1%nat) a)

Definition int_iter_succ_r {A} (f : A -> A) `{IsEquiv _ _ f} (n : Int) (a : A)
  : int_iter f (int_succ n) a = int_iter f n (f a).

goals.conclusion: int_iter f n.+1 a = int_iter f n (f a)
int_iter f n.+1 a = int_iter f n (f a)
int_iter f 0.+1 a = int_iter f 0 (f a)
int_iter f n.+1.+1 a = int_iter f n.+1 (f a)

hypotheses.type: int_iter f (- n).+1 a = int_iter f (- n) (f a)

goals.conclusion: int_iter f (- n).-1.+1 a = int_iter f (- n).-1 (f a)

hypotheses.type: int_iter f (- n).+1 a = int_iter f (- n) (f a)

goals.conclusion: int_iter f (- 0%nat).-1.+1 a = int_iter f (- 0%nat).-1 (f a)

hypotheses.type: int_iter f (- 0%nat).+1 a = int_iter f (- 0%nat) (f a)

goals.conclusion: int_iter f (- n.+1%nat).-1.+1 a = int_iter f (- n.+1%nat).-1 (f a)

hypotheses.type: int_iter f (- n.+1%nat).+1 a = int_iter f (- n.+1%nat) (f a)

Definition int_iter_pred_l {A} (f : A -> A) `{IsEquiv _ _ f} (n : Int) (a : A)
  : int_iter f (int_pred n) a = f^-1 (int_iter f n a).

goals.conclusion: int_iter f n.-1 a = f^-1 (int_iter f n a)
int_iter f n.-1 a = f^-1 (int_iter f n a)

contents: (* Convert the problem to be a problem about [f^-1] and [-n]. *)

Definition int_iter_pred_r {A} (f : A -> A) `{IsEquiv _ _ f} (n : Int) (a : A)
  : int_iter f (int_pred n) a = int_iter f n (f^-1 a).

goals.conclusion: int_iter f n.-1 a = int_iter f n (f^-1 a)

Definition int_iter_add {A} (f : A -> A) `{IsEquiv _ _ f} (m n : Int)
  : int_iter f (m + n) == int_iter f m o int_iter f n.

goals.conclusion: int_iter f (m + n) == int_iter f m o int_iter f n
int_iter f (m + n) == int_iter f m o int_iter f n
int_iter f (m + n) a = int_iter f m (int_iter f n a)
int_iter f (0 + n) a = int_iter f 0 (int_iter f n a)

goals.conclusion: f^-1 (int_iter f (- m + n) a) = f^-1 (int_iter f (- m) (int_iter f n a))

contents: (** In particular, homotopic maps have homotopic iterations. *)

Definition int_iter_homotopic (n : Int) {A} (f f' : A -> A) `{!IsEquiv f} `{!IsEquiv f'}
  (h : f == f')
  : int_iter f n == int_iter f' n
  := int_iter_commute_map f f' idmap h n.
(** [int_iter f n x] doesn't depend on the proof that [f] is an equivalence. *)

Definition int_iter_invariant (n : Int) {A} (f : A -> A) `{!IsEquiv f}
  (P : A -> Type)
  (Psucc : forall x, P x -> P (f x))
  (Ppred : forall x, P x -> P (f^-1 x))
  : forall x, P x -> P (int_iter f n x).

goals.conclusion: forall x : A, P x -> P (int_iter f n x)

hypotheses.type: forall x : A, P x -> P (f x)
forall x : A, P x -> P (f^-1 x)

goals.conclusion: forall x : A, P x -> P (int_iter f n x)

hypotheses.type: forall x : A, P x -> P (f x)
forall x : A, P x -> P (f^-1 x)

contents:
Definition loopexp {A : Type} {x : A} (p : x = x) (z : Int) : (x = x)
  := int_iter (equiv_concat_r p x) z idpath.

Definition loopexp_succ_r {A : Type} {x : A} (p : x = x) (z : Int)
  : loopexp p z.+1 = loopexp p z @ p
  := int_iter_succ_l _ _ _.

Definition loopexp_pred_r {A : Type} {x : A} (p : x = x) (z : Int)
  : loopexp p z.-1 = loopexp p z @ p^
  := int_iter_pred_l _ _ _.

Definition loopexp_succ_l {A : Type} {x : A} (p : x = x) (z : Int)
  : loopexp p z.+1 = p @ loopexp p z.

goals.conclusion: loopexp p z.+1 = p @ loopexp p z
loopexp p z.+1 = p @ loopexp p z
loopexp p z @ p = p @ loopexp p z
loopexp p 0 @ p = p @ loopexp p 0
loopexp p z.+1 @ p = p @ loopexp p z.+1

hypotheses.type: loopexp p z @ p = p @ loopexp p z

goals.conclusion: loopexp p (- z).-1 @ p = p @ loopexp p (- z).-1

hypotheses.type: loopexp p (- z) @ p = p @ loopexp p (- z)

goals.conclusion: loopexp p 0 @ p = p @ loopexp p 0
loopexp p z.+1 @ p = p @ loopexp p z.+1

hypotheses.type: loopexp p z @ p = p @ loopexp p z

goals.conclusion: (loopexp p z @ p) @ p = p @ (loopexp p z @ p)

hypotheses.type: loopexp p z @ p = p @ loopexp p z

goals.conclusion: (loopexp p z @ p) @ p = (p @ loopexp p z) @ p

hypotheses.type: loopexp p z @ p = p @ loopexp p z

goals.conclusion: loopexp p (- z).-1 @ p = p @ loopexp p (- z).-1

hypotheses.type: loopexp p (- z) @ p = p @ loopexp p (- z)

goals.conclusion: (loopexp p (- z) @ p^) @ p = p @ (loopexp p (- z) @ p^)

hypotheses.type: loopexp p (- z) @ p = p @ loopexp p (- z)

goals.conclusion: loopexp p (- z) = p @ (loopexp p (- z) @ p^)

hypotheses.type: loopexp p (- z) @ p = p @ loopexp p (- z)

goals.conclusion: loopexp p (- z) = (p @ loopexp p (- z)) @ p^

hypotheses.type: loopexp p (- z) @ p = p @ loopexp p (- z)

goals.conclusion: loopexp p z.-1 = p^ @ loopexp p z

Definition ap_loopexp {A B} (f : A -> B) {x : A} (p : x = x) (z : Int)
  : ap f (loopexp p z) = loopexp (ap f p) z.

goals.conclusion: ap f (loopexp p z) = loopexp (ap f p) z
ap f (loopexp p z) = loopexp (ap f p) z

(fun x0 : x = x => ap f (equiv_concat_r p x x0)) ==
(fun x0 : x = x => equiv_concat_r (ap f p) (f x) (ap f x0))

  : loopexp p (m + n) = loopexp p m @ loopexp p n.

goals.conclusion: loopexp p (m + n) = loopexp p m @ loopexp p n

goals.conclusion: p^ @ loopexp p (- m + n) = (p^ @ loopexp p (- m)) @ loopexp p n

hypotheses.type: loopexp p (- m + n) = loopexp p (- m) @ loopexp p n

contents: (** Under univalence, exponentiation of loops corresponds to iteration of auto-equivalences. *)

Definition equiv_path_loopexp {A : Type} (p : A = A) (z : Int) (a : A)
  : equiv_path A A (loopexp p z) a = int_iter (equiv_path A A p) z a.

goals.conclusion: equiv_path A A (loopexp p z) a = int_iter (equiv_path A A p) z a
equiv_path A A (loopexp p z) a = int_iter (equiv_path A A p) z a

contents: refine (int_iter_commute_map _ _ (fun p => equiv_path A A p a) _ _ _).

goals.conclusion:
(fun x : A = A => equiv_path A A (equiv_concat_r p A x) a) ==
(fun x : A = A => equiv_path A A p (equiv_path A A x a))
transport idmap (q @ p) a = transport idmap p (transport idmap q a)

contents:
Definition loopexp_path_universe `{Univalence} {A : Type} (f : A <~> A)
  (z : Int) (a : A)
  : transport idmap (loopexp (path_universe f) z) a = int_iter f z a.

goals.conclusion: transport idmap (loopexp (path_universe f) z) a = int_iter f z a
transport idmap (loopexp (path_universe f) z) a = int_iter f z a

forall f : A <~> A,
transport idmap (loopexp (path_universe f) z) a = int_iter f z a

transport idmap (loopexp (path_universe (equiv_path A A p)) z) a =
int_iter (equiv_path A A p) z a

transport idmap (loopexp (path_universe (equiv_path A A p)) z) a =
equiv_path A A (loopexp p z) a

contents: refine (ap (fun q => equiv_path A A (loopexp q z) a) _).

(** ** Converting between integers and naturals *)

(** [int_of_nat] preserves successors. *)
(** [int_of_nat] preserves addition. Hence is a monoid homomorphism. *)

Definition int_nat_add (n m : nat)
  : (n + m)%int = (n + m)%nat :> Int.

Definition int_nat_sub (n m : nat)
  : (m <= n)%nat -> (n - m)%int = (n - m)%nat :> Int.
(** [int_of_nat] preserves multiplication. This makes [int_of_nat] a semiring homomorphism. *)

Definition int_nat_mul (n m : nat)
  :  (n * m)%int = (n * m)%nat :> Int.
